name: Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad
title: Ruthless Governor of Kufa
shortDescription: The ruthless governor of Kufa who suppressed Muslim ibn Aqeel's uprising through terror and sent a massive army to kill Imam Hussain (AS).
fullStory:
Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad (known as Ibn Marjana) was the cruel governor of Basra who was also given the governorship of Kufa after Yazid learned of the Kufans' uprising.

He entered Kufa with a covered face so people would think he was Imam Hussain (AS). Upon entering, through terror, mass arrests of Shias, and bribing tribal leaders, he turned the tide in Kufa and brutally martyred Muslim ibn Aqeel and Hani ibn Urwa.

After the Imam moved towards Kufa, Ibn Ziyad sent Hurr ibn Yazid to intercept the Imam, and then through threats and bribes, dispatched a 30,000-strong army under Umar ibn Sa'd to Karbala, issuing clear orders to block the water and kill the Imam.

After Ashura, in his palace, he disrespected the Imam's severed head and humiliated the Ahl al-Bayt captives. Six years later, Ibn Ziyad was killed in a battle with Ibrahim ibn Malik al-Ashtar on the banks of the Khazir River, and his foul head was sent to Mukhtar and Imam Sajjad (AS).
quoteArabic: أُقْتُلُوا الحُسَيْنَ... وَإِنْ لَمْ يَفْعَلْ عُمَرُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ فَاضْرِبُوا عُنُقَهُ...
quote: (His order to Shimr:) Tell Umar Sa'd to kill Hussain; if he refuses, behead him and take command of the army yourself.
